lever.summary: Detect terminal single-output scalar I64 Add chains and accumulate them with a direct state-machine loop instead of per-equation HashMap lookup plus generic primitive evaluation.

isomorphism.ordering: Accepted chains evaluate equations in source order and only when each Add consumes the immediately previous output.
isomorphism.tie_breaking: Accepted chains have no competing ready equations; visible intermediates, nonterminal outputs, non-Add primitives, params, effects, sub-jaxprs, and multi-output programs fall back to the existing scheduler.
isomorphism.floating_point: No floating-point equations are accepted by the fast path.
isomorphism.integer_semantics: I64 addition uses wrapping_add, matching the existing runtime overflow behavior and verified with i64::MAX.
isomorphism.errors: Missing inputs and malformed equations keep the existing fail-closed scheduler/evaluator path.

focused_tests.command: RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=1 RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/frankenjax-pass67-backend-tests cargo test -p fj-backend-cpu dependency_scheduler_direct_i64_add_chain -- --nocapture
